info.name: WooPrice API
info.description:
Complete API collection for WooPrice Sync — 28 endpoints.

**Setup:**
1. Set collection variable `base_url` to your server (e.g. `https://woo.softpple.business`).
2. Run `POST /api/auth/login` and copy the token into the collection variable `token`.
3. All other requests use `{{token}}` automatically.

**Auth levels:**
- `None` — no token required
- `User` — any valid token
- `Admin` — token must belong to a user listed in `SUPER_ADMIN_USERS` env var

request.description:
Auth: None

Verifies Nextcloud credentials. Returns a JWT (24h expiry).

Copy `token` from the response into the collection variable.

Auth: User

Returns the current user's username and role.

Response: `{"sub": "username", "role": "admin|user", "exp": 1234567890}`

Auth: None

Quick liveness check. Returns WC and Nextcloud URLs configured.

Response: `{"status": "ok", "wc_url": "...", "nextcloud_url": "..."}`

Auth: Admin

Returns app configuration (WC URL, cache TTL, auto-fetch interval). Does not expose secrets.

Auth: User

Returns login, fetch, and apply events with username, timestamp, and IP.

Query params:
- `limit` (int, default 200)

Auth: Admin — INTERNAL USE ONLY

Downloads the Nextcloud spreadsheet and returns the first 12 rows × 8 columns as raw cell values. Use this to diagnose column mapping issues.

Do not expose this endpoint publicly.

Auth: User

Returns price-change alarm thresholds. `category_id: null` means the global default applies to all categories.

Response: `[{"id": 1, "category_id": null, "threshold_percent": 20.0}]`

Auth: Admin

Replaces all alarm thresholds. Send an array — `category_id: null` sets the global default.

Request body: `[{"category_id": int|null, "threshold_percent": float}]`

Auth: User

Returns stat card data: total syncs, latest job, product stock counts, 30-day sync chart, recent audit log entries.

Auth: User

Returns three product lists based on the most recent SyncItem per product:
- `in_stock_no_price` — in stock but no price set
- `has_price_out_of_stock` — price set but out of stock
- `stale_products` — not updated in over 7 days

Auth: User

Returns WooCommerce categories (cached 5 min). Serves stale cache if WooCommerce is temporarily unavailable.

Response: `[{"id": 1, "name": "تبلت اپل", "slug": "apple-tablet"}]`

Auth: User

Status of the in-memory WooCommerce product cache (used for individual price lookups).

Response includes `age_seconds`, `ttl_hours`, `auto_fetch_hours`.

Auth: Admin

Clears the in-memory WooCommerce product cache. Next preview will re-fetch from WooCommerce.

Auth: User

Returns all products stored in the local DB cache (`products_cache` table).

Run `GET /api/fetch/full` first to populate. Returns `{"stats": {...}, "products": [...]}`.

Auth: User

Returns `{"total": N, "last_synced_at": "ISO-datetime"}`. `total` is 0 until a full sync is run.

Auth: Admin

Deletes all rows from the `products_cache` DB table. Next preview will re-fetch all products from WooCommerce.

Auth: User

Updates a single product price in WooCommerce AND writes back to the Nextcloud spreadsheet.

Request body:
- `new_price` (str, required) — new price as a decimal string
- `parent_id` (int, default 0) — set if this is a variation
- `job_id` (int|null) — if provided, also updates the SyncItem in DB

Response: `{"success": true, "product_id": N, "new_price": "299.99"}`

Auth: User

Updates a single product's stock status and/or quantity in WooCommerce.

Request body:
- `stock_status` (str, required) — `"instock"` or `"outofstock"`
- `stock_quantity` (int|null) — if set, also enables `manage_stock`
- `parent_id` (int, default 0) — set if this is a variation
- `job_id` (int|null) — if provided, also updates the SyncItem in DB

Response: `{"success": true, "product_id": N}`

Auth: User (query-string token)

Streams a full WooCommerce product sync into the local DB cache. Fetches ALL products and variations via paginated WC REST API.

SSE event format: `data: {"step": "...", "status": "running|ok|error", "msg": "..."}`

Expected sequence:
1. `{"step":"start", "status":"running"}`
2. `{"step":"fetch", "status":"running", "msg":"Fetched N products..."}`
3. `{"step":"done", "status":"ok", "msg":"Cache updated: X new, Y updated"}`

Run this once after deployment to populate the cache. After that, use Light Sync for incremental updates.

⚠️ Postman does not render SSE streams live. View raw response body for the event lines.

Auth: User (query-string token)

Streams a light sync — fetches only products modified after `last_synced_at` from the DB cache.

Requires: a full sync must have been run first. Returns `error` if no prior sync found.

Same SSE event format as Full Sync.

Auth: User

Downloads the Nextcloud spreadsheet, looks up products from DB cache (falls back to WooCommerce for missing IDs), and returns a full preview result in one HTTP response.

Use `GET /api/preview/stream` instead for real-time progress.

Response: `{"job_id": N, "total": N, "changed_count": N, "unchanged_count": N, "items": [...]}`

Item fields: `product_id`, `product_name` (Column A), `sku`, `old_price`, `new_price`, `sale_price`, `stock_status`, `stock_quantity`, `categories`, `row_color`, `changed` (bool), `last_price_updated`, `wc_date_modified`

Auth: User (query-string token)

Streams the full preview flow with live progress events:
1. `{"step":"excel", "status":"running"}` — downloading spreadsheet
2. `{"step":"excel", "status":"done", "msg":"Found N products"}`
3. `{"step":"wc", "status":"running"}` — fetching missing products
4. `{"step":"wc", "status":"done"}`
5. `{"step":"calc", "status":"running"}` — calculating diffs
6. `{"step":"calc", "status":"done"}`
7. `{"step":"preview", "status":"done", "job_id": N, "items": [...]}` — full result

Also sends `: keepalive` comments every 10s while fetching WooCommerce to prevent proxy timeout.

Product names in items use Column A as authoritative source, falling back to WooCommerce name.

Auth: User

Applies a preview job — pushes all changed prices to WooCommerce via batch API, updates parent stock statuses.

Job must be in `preview` status. Returns immediately with final counts.

Response: `{"job_id": N, "status": "completed", "updated": N, "failed": N, "skipped": N}`

Errors: `404` job not found, `400` wrong status, `502` WooCommerce batch failed.

query.description: Product ID to include in this apply (repeat for each)

request.description:
Auth: User (query-string token)

Streams the apply operation for selected product IDs (`?sid=` repeated). Emits one event per product as it is updated.

SSE event types:
- `{"type":"item", "product_id":N, "product_name":"...", "status":"updated|failed|skipped", "old_price":"...", "new_price":"...", "error":""}`
- `{"type":"done", "job_id":N, "updated":N, "failed":N, "skipped":N}`
- `{"type":"error", "msg":"..."}`

Auth: User

Cancels a preview job (sets status to `cancelled`). Only works on jobs with status `preview`.

Response: `{"job_id": N, "status": "cancelled"}`

Errors: `404` not found, `400` job is not in preview status.

Auth: User

Returns the most recent sync jobs ordered by creation time descending.

Each job: `{id, created_at, completed_at, status, total_count, updated_count, failed_count, skipped_count}`

Statuses: `preview | running | completed | failed | cancelled`

Auth: User

Returns full job details including all SyncItems.

Response: job object + `items` array. Each item includes product_id, product_name (Column A), sku, old_price, new_price, stock_status, status (pending|updated|failed|skipped), error_message, synced_at.

Auth: User

Writes sync results back to the Nextcloud spreadsheet:
- Column E: status (updated/failed/skipped)
- Column F: synced_at timestamp
- Column G: error message

Job must be in `completed` status.

Errors: `404` not found, `400` job not completed, `502` Nextcloud upload failed.
